title: Ensure Cloud Object Storage Encryption is set to On with  KYOK

input_row.title: Ensure Cloud Object Storage Encryption is set to On with  KYOK
input_row.description: You can use IBM Cloud encryption key management services, for example, Hyper Protect Crypto Services to keep and manage exclusive control over the root keys used to add envelop encryption for data that is stored in IBM Cloud Object Storage buckets.
input_row.rationale: When it comes to encryption of data at rest in IBM Cloud, the best practice is to allow customers use encryption capabilities offered by Hardware Security Module crypto service. IBM Cloud Hyper Protect Crypto Services offers Keep your Own Key (KYOK) capabilities that allow customers to have exclusive key control over encryption keys. This provides a significant level of control over encryption keys where only authorized users have access (no privileged users included IBM Cloud Admins) to encryption keys, in turn increases both security control as well as meet relevant compliance requirements.
input_row.audit: You can use the IBM Cloud Object Storage bucket configuration properties to verify whether a Cloud Object Storage bucket is configured to use Hyper Protect Crypto Services key management service. Navigate to your Cloud Object Storage instance: 1. Log in to IBM Cloud at https://cloud.ibm.com 2. Click the Menu icon and select Resource List 3. On the Resource List page under Storage, select the Cloud Object Storage instance that you have provisioned. 4. Click on the appropriate bucket that you would like to check 5. Click on Configuration and scroll down to Associated key management service to check/confirm if Hyper Protect Crypto Services key is configured with the bucket
input_row.remediation: You will not be able to add Hyper Protect Crypto Services as the key management service once data is already written to a Cloud Object Storage bucket. In order to ensure that objects are encrypted using Hyper Protect Crypto Services root keys you will need to create a new Cloud Object Storage bucket, set it to use Hyper Protect Crypto Services key management service and then upload/copy the existing objects to this new bucket.
input_row.impact: For Keep your Own Key (KYOK) encryption using IBM Cloud Hyper Protect Crypto Services key management service you will need to review and understand how key lifecycle events (for example key rotation) need to be handled. Refer to the Hyper Protect Crypto Services product page for details around the key lifecycle events supported.
input_row.references: 1. • Data encryption with IBM Cloud Object Storage: https://cloud.ibm.com/docs/cloud-object-storage?topic=cloud-object-storage- encryption 2. • Integrated service availability page: https://cloud.ibm.com/docs/services/cloud- object-storage/basics?topic=cloud-object-storage-service-availability
input_row.default_value: By default, use of Hyper Protect Crypto Services key management service with IBM Cloud Object Storage buckets is not enabled.
input_row.additional_information: Create a Cloud Object Storage bucket with Hyper Protect Crypto Services (Keep Your Own Key): 1. From your Cloud Object Storage instance page, create a new Cloud Object Storage bucket by clicking Create bucket. 2. To create the bucket, in the Create bucket section, select Custom bucket. 3. In the bucket configuration section, you can select the option to use Hyper Protect Crypto Services under Key management services Note: In order to configure a Cloud Object Storage bucket to use Hyper Protect Crypto Services you will need an instance of Hyper Protect Crypto Services along with appropriate access authorization to your Cloud Object Storage instance.

step1_initial_assessment:
**AUDIT APPROACH:** Automated

**APPROACH DECISION:**
The control specifies a configuration property of IBM Cloud Object Storage buckets that can be queried through IBM Cloud APIs. The audit steps provided in the audit field outline a verification process that can translate into API checks and CLI commands. Thus, the issues related to the encryption status can be effectively automated.

**PROGRAM NAME:** ibm_cos_bucket_encryption_at_rest_enabled

**AUTOMATION DETAILS:**
1. **Use the IBM Cloud CLI command** `ibmcloud cos get-bucket-encryption --bucket <bucket-name> --region <region>` to verify if the bucket encryption is enabled with Hyper Protect Crypto Services.
    - This command specifically checks the encryption settings of the specified bucket within the given region to confirm the key management service in use.
    
2. **Use another CLI command** to list all buckets under the Cloud Object Storage instance: `ibmcloud cos list-buckets --region <region>`.
    - This command will provide a list of buckets along with their metadata, which can then be used to verify encryption settings for each listed bucket.

3. **Pass/Fail Criteria:**
    - **Pass:** The output of the `get-bucket-encryption` command shows that the bucket is using Hyper Protect Crypto Services (KYOK).
    - **Fail:** The output indicates that either the bucket does not use encryption or is using a different key management service.

4. **Additional Validation:**
    - If the bucket returns a configuration where KYOK is not enabled, it should also check the date when the bucket was created, as the remediation suggests creating a new bucket if data already exists in the original bucket.

This approach allows for efficient and thorough verification of encryption settings on IBM Cloud Object Storage buckets to ensure compliance with the specified security control.
step2_review:
**APPROACH VALIDATION:** AGREE
- The classification of the audit approach as Automated is correct. The control can be effectively checked using IBM Cloud CLI commands, which align with the capabilities provided by IBM Cloud APIs and SDKs. The use of CLI commands to query encryption settings is appropriate for automation.

**TECHNICAL ACCURACY:** 8
- The automation details are mostly technically correct. The `ibmcloud cos get-bucket-encryption` command is valid for checking the encryption settings of a bucket. However, the command should explicitly mention the use of Hyper Protect Crypto Services (KYOK) for clarity.
- The `ibmcloud cos list-buckets` command is correctly used to gather a list of buckets for further inspection.
- The program name `ibm_cos_bucket_encryption_at_rest_enabled` is descriptive but could be more specific by including the encryption method, e.g., `ibm_cos_bucket_encryption_kyok_enabled`.

**IMPROVEMENTS:**
- The program name could be improved to `ibm_cos_bucket_encryption_kyok_at_rest_enabled` to clearly indicate that the check is for KYOK encryption at rest.
- Consider adding a step to verify the specific encryption key management service being used by parsing the output of the `get-bucket-encryption` command to ensure it explicitly states Hyper Protect Crypto Services.
- The additional validation step regarding the creation date of the bucket is a good consideration but could be expanded to include a recommendation for transitioning data securely if a new bucket is required.
- Alternative methods could include using IBM Cloud SDKs for more programmatic checks or integrating with IBM Cloud Config for continuous monitoring.

**CONFIDENCE:** HIGH
- The assessment is well-founded in the use of IBM Cloud CLI commands and aligns with IBM Cloud's capabilities for automating compliance checks.

**FINAL RECOMMENDATION:**
- Modify: Update the program name to `ibm_cos_bucket_encryption_kyok_at_rest_enabled` for clarity.
- Ensure the automation script explicitly checks for Hyper Protect Crypto Services in the encryption settings output.
- Consider expanding the additional validation step to provide guidance on secure data transition if a new bucket is needed.

step3_final_decision.decision_text:
**FINAL APPROACH:** Automated

**JUSTIFICATION:**
Both AI opinions agree that the audit approach can be automated using IBM Cloud CLI commands, which are capable of querying the encryption settings of Cloud Object Storage buckets. The IBM Cloud CLI provides the necessary commands to verify if the encryption is set to use Hyper Protect Crypto Services, aligning with the compliance control requirements. The technical capabilities of IBM Cloud APIs support this automated approach effectively.

**CONFIDENCE:** HIGH

---

**PROGRAM NAME:** 
ibm_cos_bucket_encryption_kyok_at_rest_enabled

**AUTOMATION DETAILS:**
- IBM Cloud Resource/Service: Cloud Object Storage
- API/CLI Method: IBM Cloud CLI `ibmcloud cos get-bucket-encryption --bucket <bucket-name> --region <region>`
- Properties to Check: Encryption settings indicating the use of Hyper Protect Crypto Services (KYOK)
- Pass Criteria: The output of the `get-bucket-encryption` command confirms the bucket is using Hyper Protect Crypto Services for encryption.
- Fail Criteria: The output indicates that the bucket is not using Hyper Protect Crypto Services or is not encrypted.

---

**DISAGREEMENTS RESOLVED:**
There were no significant disagreements between the AI opinions; both supported an automated approach. The second opinion suggested a more descriptive program name, which has been adopted for clarity.

**SOURCE:** CIS_IBM_Cloud_Foundations_Benchmark_v1_1_0
